Diary
  1. Home
  2. Activities
  3. Diary
  4. Paul San Martin | Friday Blues
  • 05 Jun 26
    upcoming

    Paul San Martin | Friday Blues

    Friday, 5 June 2026 - 18:00

    Advance ticket sales: 18.00€ / Price at the entrance: 18.00€

    Tickets Personalize your visit
    • Description

    Paul was born in San Sebastián in 1979. Holding a higher degree in jazz piano, his great passion was and continues to be the blues. His first contact with this style came through British rhythm & blues bands of the 1960s. He has been performing since the age of 15 and is currently fully immersed in the pianistic tradition of blues, mastering styles such as “stride” and “boogie woogie.” In 2011 he released his first solo album, “Tracks,” on the Gaztelupeko Hotsak label, a piano-and-vocals recording.

    Among his influences are musicians such as Alan Price, Jerry Lee Lewis, Roosevelt Sykes, Memphis Slim, Otis Spann, Sunnyland Slim, Dr. John, Professor Longhair, and James Booker.

    Throughout his extensive career, Paul San Martín has collaborated with highly relevant artists, including Nico Wayne Toussaint, Otis Grand, Gerry Hundt, Lluís Coloma, James Armstrong, Ñaco Goñi, Slam Allen, Sax Gordon, Chris Ruest, and the legendary blues figure Billy Boy Arnold. In 2013 he released another piano-and-vocals album, “Paul’s Mood,” again with Gaztelupeko Hotsak, a new pianistic immersion into different blues styles.

    In 2023 he won the European Blues Challenge in Chorzów (Poland) together with the band Noa & The Hell Drinkers.

    Paul comes directly from San Sebastián to delight the MEAM audience.

    Blues & Boogie with PAUL SAN MARTÍN

    • PAUL SAN MARTÍN, piano, vocals

    FAQS

    • All tickets include access to the exhibitions, the concert, and one beer. You must arrive between 5:00 pm and 5:50 pm.
    • Seats are assigned according to the order in which bookings are made. Once seats have been assigned, changes will not be possible.
    • Access to the concert hall will take place from 5:40 pm to 6:55 pm. Once the concert has started, entry to the hall will no longer be permitted.
    • The exhibition must be visited before the concert. We recommend arriving around 5:00 pm.
    • The exhibition tour lasts approximately 45 minutes.
    • There is no need to print your ticket.
    • The museum reserves the right to cancel unpaid bookings 5 minutes before the concert begins. For this reason, we recommend arriving at the ticket office 30 minutes in advance.
    • For tickets purchased in advance: after 6:00 pm, refunds will no longer be available. If the ticket holder has not informed the venue before the concert about any delay or inability to attend, the tickets may be reassigned to another user from 6:00 pm onwards.
This event is framed within

Friday Blues

Concerts

Every friday, the museum turns into a hub of art, rhythm, and good vibes. Our Friday Blues series is more than just a concert — it’s a full-on experience...
